findings.evidence_quote: ~37% of filers qualify. [Simple Form 1040 returns only](https://turbotax.intuit.com/reviews/online/free-edition/#modals/simple-tax-returns-en) (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A).
findings.location: hero section, directly under 'TurboTax Free Edition reviews' headline and intro text
findings.reasoning: Trigger: The page prominently mentions TurboTax Free Edition in the title, headline, and review context, so R-01 is triggered. Requirement: The rule requires that right underneath any mention of TurboTax Free, the '~37% of filers qualify...' disclosure appear. Here, directly beneath the hero headline/intro ('Read unbiased reviews from real TurboTax Free Edition customers'), the exact disclosure text appears verbatim, matching the approved D-01 library text word-for-word including the hyperlink structure. Axis A: satisfied - disclosure present and adjacent to the free mention. Axis B: matches approval exactly, verbatim ('~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A)'), so axis B is true. Counter-reading considered: one could argue the placement is technically below the page title but interposed by a subheading only two lines above - this is a minor gap, not different paragraph, and reads as immediately following the free-edition mention, so I still judge it as satisfying 'right underneath'. Overall: all_good, pass.

findings.evidence_quote: TurboTax Free Edition: TurboTax Free Edition ($0 Federal + $0 State + $0 To File) is available for those filing simple Form 1040 returns only (no forms or schedules except as needed to claim the Earned Income Tax Credit, Child Tax Credit, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A). More details are available here. Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify. Offer may change or end at any time without notice.
findings.location: OFFERS & PRICING footer section, 'TurboTax Free Edition' bullet
findings.reasoning: Trigger: This is a second, separate mention of TurboTax Free Edition within the page's footer legal disclosures (pricing/offers section), which itself triggers R-01 independently. Requirement: the substantive content (eligibility percentage + simple 1040 limitation with EITC/CTC/student loan interest/Schedule 1-A carve-outs) is present, satisfying the substance of the rule, so axis A is compliant. However, the wording deviates from the approved D-01 text: 'Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify' instead of '~37% of filers qualify', and the phrasing order differs (limitation stated before percentage, rather than immediately underneath in the approved format). This mirrors Worked Example 1 exactly. Axis B: false due to wording drift. Counter-reading: one might argue this bullet is deep within legal boilerplate, not a top-level 'mention' of free requiring the disclosure immediately beneath it, but the bullet explicitly names 'TurboTax Free Edition' as a pricing/offer item, which is a mention of Free, so the rule is triggered here too. Given axis A yes / axis B no, this is drifted_but_compliant, verdict flag.

findings.evidence_quote:
TurboTax Free Edition reviews
Read unbiased reviews from real TurboTax Free Edition customers.
~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A).
findings.location: Top of page, under main reviews header
findings.reasoning: Rule R-01 is triggered because the page clearly mentions TurboTax Free Edition, i.e., TurboTax Free. The rule requires that immediately underneath such a mention, the specific eligibility disclosure be present. Directly under the TurboTax Free Edition reviews heading and description, the page displays the exact approved disclosure: "~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A)." This matches the approved library text D-01 verbatim, including the tilde, "filers" term, and the Simple Form 1040 limitation language and exceptions. Placement is directly underneath the Free Edition mention, satisfying the positional requirement. Though elsewhere on the page there are slightly different phrasings (e.g., "Roughly 37% of taxpayers are eligible" and "Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify"), the rule only requires that when TurboTax Free is mentioned, the specified disclosure be present right underneath. That condition is fulfilled for the key Free Edition mention at the top of the page with exact wording, so Axis A is compliant and Axis B matches approval. Counter-reading considered: that later, drifted percentage language in the disclosures section might render the page non-compliant. However, R-01 is scoped to the disclosure that must accompany a TurboTax Free mention, and that required disclosure is correctly implemented near the primary Free Edition reference, so the page passes under this rule.

findings.evidence_quote: * TurboTax Free Edition: TurboTax Free Edition ($0 Federal + $0 State + $0 To File) is available for those filing simple Form 1040 returns only (no forms or schedules except as needed to claim the Earned Income Tax Credit, Child Tax Credit, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A). More details are available here. Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify.
findings.reasoning: This second distinct mention of "TurboTax Free Edition" in the Offers & Pricing section also triggers R-01. The rule requires that, when TurboTax Free is mentioned, the specific eligibility disclosure text be provided right underneath: "~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A)." Here, the copy includes substantively equivalent elements: it states the simple Form 1040 limitation with the same exceptions and includes a percentage eligibility statement ("Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify."). This satisfies the core requirement that the ~37% eligibility and simple-return-only limitation be disclosed in connection with the Free Edition reference, so Axis A is substantively compliant. However, the wording diverges from the approved library entry: it uses "Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify" instead of "~37% of filers qualify," and includes extra phrasing like the detailed $0 pricing and a "More details are available here" link. Under the policy definitions, any such drift from the approved wording makes Axis B false. The strongest counter-reading is that because all required concepts are clearly communicated and are in close proximity, we might treat this as fully compliant. But the instructions and worked example explicitly treat this exact style of change ("Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify") as drift, so this instance must be labeled drifted_but_compliant and flagged.

findings.evidence_quote: ~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A).
findings.location: Top intro beneath page title/heading for TurboTax Free Edition reviews
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because the page is about TurboTax Free Edition and promotes it. The required disclosure appears immediately underneath and matches D-01 exactly. Axis A is satisfied (right underneath; includes % and Simple 1040 limitation). Axis B matches the approved library verbatim. Counter-reading: one might argue placement is not exactly “underneath” the first Free mention; however, the disclosure line directly follows the heading/intro where Free Edition is referenced, satisfying the rule’s placement intent.

findings.evidence_quote: If you have a simple Form 1040 return only (no forms or schedules except as needed to claim the Earned Income Tax Credit, Child Tax Credit, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A), you can file for free yourself with TurboTax Free Edition, or you can file with TurboTax Expert Assist Basic at the listed price. Roughly 37% of taxpayers are eligible.
findings.location: Section: “TurboTax Online: Important Details about Filing Simple Form 1040 Returns”
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because it explicitly says you can file for free with TurboTax Free Edition. The paragraph includes both the Simple 1040 limitation and an eligibility percentage, so Axis A is satisfied. However, the wording drifts from D-01 (“Roughly 37% of taxpayers are eligible” vs “~37% of filers qualify” and “no forms or schedules” vs “no schedules”), so Axis B is not met. Counter-reading: one could argue this is explanatory content and not required to carry the exact disclosure; still, the rule applies whenever TurboTax Free is mentioned, and the presence of the disclosure elements confirms the intent, but the copy is not verbatim-equivalent to D-01.

findings.evidence_quote: TurboTax Free Edition: TurboTax Free Edition ($0 Federal + $0 State + $0 To File) is available for those filing simple Form 1040 returns only (no forms or schedules except as needed to claim the Earned Income Tax Credit, Child Tax Credit, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A). More details are available here. Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify. Offer may change or end at any time without notice.
findings.location: Section: “TurboTax Online/MOBILE OFFERS & PRICING” bullet for TurboTax Free Edition
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because the bullet promotes TurboTax Free Edition. It contains the Simple 1040 limitation and an eligibility percentage, so Axis A is met. But the phrasing deviates from D-01 (“no forms or schedules,” “Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify”), so Axis B fails, yielding drifted_but_compliant. Counter-reading: one could claim the bullet’s integrated disclosure satisfies both the substance and format; however, the rule’s Axis B requires verbatim-or-equivalent library wording, which this does not match.
